additional_findings.merge_markers_everywhere: 6 workflow files have committed `<<<<<<< HEAD` / `=======` / `>>>>>>> origin/main` blocks (codeql, quality-gate, doc-links, fr-coverage, legacy-tooling-gate, publish-package). The pattern is uniform: HEAD side is a stripped-down `on: workflow_dispatch:`, origin/main side is the real schedule + concurrency. Looks like a botched merge of a workflow-simplification PR against main. ALL of these workflows are NON-FUNCTIONAL — GitHub Actions will refuse to parse them. This is the single most blocking issue in the repo.
additional_findings.empty_link_checker: scripts/check_docs_links.py is 0 bytes. The 354-line tests/test_check_docs_links.py imports 8 symbols from it. deploy.yml:55 (`bun run check`) and package.json:15 (`check`) call this empty file. The most-cited gate in this repo runs an empty file.
additional_findings.duplicate_markdownlint_config: .markdownlint.json (14 lines) AND .markdownlint.yaml (25 lines) both present — conflicting rule formats, only one is read by markdownlint-cli. Delete one (json preferred for compactness).
additional_findings.duplicate_top_level_keys: 5 workflows have duplicate top-level keys (permissions/concurrency/on) — scorecard.yml:13/17, workflow-permissions.yml:9/12, deploy.yml:2/13 + 8/19, alert-sync-issues.yml:2/13. YAML parser takes the last one, but it's sloppy and signals unmerged bot PRs.
additional_findings.invalid_setup_python_syntax: security-guard.yml:27 `actions/setup-python@a309ff8b426b58ec0e2a45f0f869d46889d02405@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065` — TWO @ separators. The SHA a309ff8b... is the real pin; the trailing `@a26af69b...` is an invalid comment that GitHub silently ignores. Drift from org canonical setup-python@c8813ba1.
additional_findings.broken_md_refs_committed: .broken_md_refs.txt is real scan output of 335 broken md links (CONTRIBUTING/SPEC/ADR-* pointing to non-existent or moved files). Should be in .airlock/ or gitignored, and a CI step (in a fixed doc-links.yml) should regenerate it.
additional_findings.package_json_drift: package.json:26 typescript ^6.0.2, vue-tsc ^3.2.6 — version numbers ahead of public release timelines (verify against registry). package.json:30 engines.node '>=20.0.0' but no .nvmrc / .node-version. license=MIT consistent with LICENSE.
additional_findings.local_licensing: LICENSE is single MIT (no LICENSE-MIT/LICENSE-APACHE dual like 14+ fleet peers). drift vs the dual-license fan-out.
additional_findings.branch_inventory_unknown: git ls-remote not run; local state not inspected beyond ls. 0 PRs visible in audit scope.

ci_status.workflows_parsing: 10/16 parse cleanly (alert-sync-issues, deploy, journey-verify, policy-gate, scorecard, security-guard, security-guard-hook-audit, sonarcloud, trufflehog, workflow-permissions). 6/16 have committed merge-conflict markers and will fail to parse in GitHub Actions (codeql, quality-gate, doc-links, fr-coverage, legacy-tooling-gate, publish-package).
ci_status.open_prs_unknown: git ls-remote not run in audit scope
ci_status.last_commit: no git log run; CI status inferred from workflow files + .git HEAD mtime

actions_recommended.title: Resolve all 6 merge-conflict markers in committed workflows (repo is non-functional in CI)
actions_recommended.detail: Six .github/workflows/*.yml files have committed `<<<<<<< HEAD` / `=======` / `>>>>>>> origin/main` blocks: codeql.yml:2-10, quality-gate.yml:2-11, doc-links.yml:2-11, fr-coverage.yml:2-11, legacy-tooling-gate.yml:2-10, publish-package.yml:2-9. The HEAD side is a stripped `on: workflow_dispatch:` (effectively disabling the schedule), the origin/main side is the real schedule + concurrency. Action: for each file, delete the merge-marker block and keep the origin/main side (which has `concurrency: cancel-in-progress` + the real triggers). After resolution, all 6 workflows will parse, the schedule triggers (codeql Mon 04:17, etc.) will resume, and the CI will become functional. Also add `pre-commit`'s `check-merge-conflict` hook to .pre-commit-config.yaml:12 (already present, good) so this can't recur. Net: closes G1 in one PR, restores 6 workflows.

actions_recommended.title: Replace empty scripts/check_docs_links.py with a real implementation + run pytest in CI
actions_recommended.detail: scripts/check_docs_links.py is 0 bytes. The 354-line tests/test_check_docs_links.py imports 8 symbols (KNOWN_PLACEHOLDERS, LinkResult, check_link, check_links_concurrent, collect_all_links, extract_links_from_file, is_placeholder_url, print_report) — all defined in the missing source. deploy.yml:55 (`bun run check`) and package.json:15 call this empty file, so the entire e2e link-check pipeline is theatre. Action: (1) restore scripts/check_docs_links.py with the 8 exported symbols that the test file imports (httpx-based async link checker per the test signatures — `check_link` takes AsyncMock client + URL, returns LinkResult dataclass with url/file_path/line_number/status_code/is_valid/error; `is_placeholder_url` matches the @pytest.mark.parametrize cases). (2) Add a `pytest tests/` step to quality-gate.yml (after fixing the merge markers) so the 2 test files (test_check_docs_links.py, test_vitepress_build.py) actually run. (3) Replace the `|| true` swallow in quality-gate.yml:30-31, doc-links.yml:37, fr-coverage.yml:37 with hard exits. Net: closes G4 + most of e2e in one PR.
actions_recommended.estimated_effort: 30 min for the script restore (test file already documents the API) + 10 min for the workflow fix = 40 min

actions_recommended.title: Delete committed .broken_md_refs.txt + gitignore + add SUPPORT.md + dedupe markdownlint config + pin trufflehog reusable
actions_recommended.detail: Five small hygiene fixes in one PR: (1) .broken_md_refs.txt is 52KB/335 lines of committed scan output (real bugs: CONTRIBUTING.md → ./CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SPEC.md → ./getting-started.md, ADR-001..008 → ../../ARCHITECTURE.md, ADR-001..005 → ../../docs/api-standards.md, docs/guide/getting-started.md → /guide/architecture.md, etc.) — `git rm` it, append `.broken_md_refs.txt` to .gitignore:33, move to .airlock/ alongside the other scan artifacts. (2) Add SUPPORT.md (heliosrouter PR #114 model). (3) Delete .markdownlint.yaml OR .markdownlint.json (duplicate config; pick the json for compactness). (4) Trufflehog.yml:13 reusable at `@main` — pin to SHA: `KooshaPari/phenotype-tooling/.github/workflows/reusable/trufflehog.yml@<sha>` (look up the org canonical pin). (5) Fix security-guard.yml:27 invalid two-`@` setup-python syntax — replace with `actions/setup-python@c8813ba1bc76ebf779b911ad8ffccbf2e449cb48 # v-latest@a26af69be951a213d495a4c3e4e4022e16d87065` (canonical org pin). (6) Calm alert-sync-issues.yml cron `17 * * * *` → `17 */6 * * *` (every 6h, ~4x cost reduction). Closes G2 + G3 + G5 + e2e in one PR.

deep_research: phenodocs is the VitePress-rendered docs site for the Phenotype fleet — a Vue 3.5 + VitePress 1.5 + Bun 1.3.10 + uv-managed Python 3.14 project that aggregates Markdown from across the org into a federation hub. It hosts 23 docs subdirs (adr, api, contributing, development, governance, guide, guides, index, journeys, operations, planning, public, quick-start, reference, research, roadmap, scripts, security, sessions, templates, views, wiki) plus a Python lib at libs/ and a workspace at packages/{design,docs,shared-utils} that publishes to GitHub Packages as `@phenotype/docs`. Comparing against the tick-25 hygiene wave, the helioslab-hygiene baseline, and the localbase3-hygiene (3.6/5) and phenodocs-scorecard-remediation-hygiene (3.6/5) audits — phenodocs itself is in worse shape: 6 of 16 workflows have committed `<<<<<<< HEAD` merge-conflict markers (codeql, quality-gate, doc-links, fr-coverage, legacy-tooling-gate, publish-package) which means GitHub Actions will refuse to parse them. The pattern is uniform — HEAD side is `on: workflow_dispatch:`, origin/main side is the real schedule + concurrency — strongly suggesting a botched merge of a workflow-simplification PR. Combined with scripts/check_docs_links.py being 0 bytes (the test file imports 8 symbols from it), the entire e2e link-check pipeline is theatre. The two structural positives are: (1) .coderabbit.yaml with `profile: assertive` + auto_review + auto_incremental_review is fleet-leading, and (2) policy-gate.yml with its fix/* → non-main enforcement + no-merge-commits check is unique in the fleet and worth keeping. The 52KB/335-line .broken_md_refs.txt committed at root is the most visible hygiene rot — it's a real scan output of 335 broken md links (CONTRIBUTING→CODE_OF_CONDUCT, SPEC→getting-started, ADR-001..008→ARCHITECTURE, etc.) and should be gitignored + regenerated by a fixed doc-links.yml on PR.
deep_research_cont: Net hygiene score: 2.5/5 (G1 1.5/5 due to P0 merge-marker rot, G2 3.5/5, G3 3.5/5, G4 2/5, G5 3.5/5, e2e 2/5). The single highest-leverage PR is the merge-marker resolution (closes 6 broken workflows in 15 min, restores the entire CI surface). The second is restoring check_docs_links.py — the test file already documents the exact API surface, so the restore is largely transcription. After both P0 fixes, phenodocs moves to 4.5/5 (G1 4.5, G4 4, e2e 4) and the e2e story becomes real (VitePress build + Pages deploy + working link check + pytest in CI). The P1 fix sweeps up the residual hygiene debt (.broken_md_refs.txt, SUPPORT.md, markdownlint dedupe, trufflehog @main, two-@ setup-python syntax, alert-sync hourly cron) and lands the repo in the same shape as heliosrouter / localbase3 / phenodocs-scorecard-remediation after their respective PRs. No git ls-remote was run in audit scope, so open PR state is unknown; 0 PRs visible locally. Local branch inventory not inspected.
